title: orobanche mutelii var. floribunda
intro: Orobanche mutelii var. floribunda, also known as the broom-rape or broom-crown, is a parasitic plant species that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of its host plant, typically from the genera of Astragalus or Lathyrus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Orobanche mutelii var. floribunda include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a diuretic, making it useful in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and bronchitis. Orobanche mutelii var. floribunda is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and has been used in various herbal remedies, including those for treating digestive issues and promoting wound healing.
